channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Venezuela reels from earthquakes as rescuers scramble to find survivors
item.description: As Venezuela begins counting the cost of its deadliest quake disaster in over a century, a shattered economy and struggling health system threaten to slow recovery efforts.
item.content:encoded:
As Venezuela begins counting the cost of its deadliest quake disaster in over a century, a shattered economy and struggling health system threaten to slow recovery efforts.

(Image credit: Pedro Mattey)
item.dc:creator: Manuel Rueda

item.title: UN agency pauses evacuation of ships through the Strait of Hormuz after attack on vessel
item.content:encoded: (Image credit: Amirhosein Khorgooi/AP Photo/Amirhosein Khorgooi)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: King Charles III will not live at Buckingham Palace after its costly refurbishment
item.description: The decision was announced Thursday during a briefing on royal finances at which Charles became the first British monarch to reveal the taxes he paid to the government.
item.content:encoded:
The decision was announced Thursday during a briefing on royal finances at which Charles became the first British monarch to reveal the taxes he paid to the government.

(Image credit: Kirsty Wigglesworth)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Photos: See Venezuela destruction after earthquakes
item.description: The earthquakes were Venezuela's largest in over a century.
item.content:encoded:
The earthquakes were Venezuela's largest in over a century.

(Image credit: Federico Parra)
item.dc:creator: John Otis

item.title: 2 earthquakes in Venezuela kill at least 164. And, Trump cancels housing bill signing
item.description: Two major earthquakes in Venezuela have killed at least 164 people and left hundreds injured. And, President Trump canceled the signing of a massive bipartisan housing bill yesterday.
item.content:encoded:
Two major earthquakes in Venezuela have killed at least 164 people and left hundreds injured. And, President Trump canceled the signing of a massive bipartisan housing bill yesterday.

(Image credit: Manaure Quintero)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: 'They can kill you': African migrants fear a surge in xenophobic violence in South Africa
item.description: Foreign-owned businesses have been attacked, African migrants driven from their homes, and several killed. A leading xenophobic group has given all undocumented immigrants until June 30 to leave the country.
item.content:encoded:
Foreign-owned businesses have been attacked, African migrants driven from their homes, and several killed. A leading xenophobic group has given all undocumented immigrants until June 30 to leave the country.

(Image credit: Themba Hadebe)
item.dc:creator: Kate Bartlett

item.title: The science behind the back-to-back earthquakes that struck northern Venezuela
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with earthquake scientist Judith Hubbard of Cornell University about the science behind the multiple earthquakes in Venezuela, Japan and northern California Wednesday.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with earthquake scientist Judith Hubbard of Cornell University about the science behind the multiple earthquakes in Venezuela, Japan and northern California Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Trump airs grievances with NATO chief over alliance's lack of Iran war support
item.description: President Trump met with NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte Wednesday, where he shared his frustrations with the lack of support he's received from allies on the Iran war.
item.content:encoded: President Trump met with NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte Wednesday, where he shared his frustrations with the lack of support he's received from allies on the Iran war.
item.dc:creator: Teri Schultz

item.title: Europe is 'reading the tea leaves' on Trump's Ukraine policy, says ex-NATO envoy
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with Kurt Volker, former U.S. ambassador to NATO, about the dynamics between the NATO alliance, its leader, and President Trump.
item.content:encoded:
NPR's Michel Martin speaks with Kurt Volker, former U.S. ambassador to NATO, about the dynamics between the NATO alliance, its leader, and President Trump.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: How is Moscow dealing with Ukraine's stepped-up drone attacks?
item.description: The view from Moscow as Russia and Russian-occupied territory contend with stepped-up Ukrainian attacks.
item.content:encoded: The view from Moscow as Russia and Russian-occupied territory contend with stepped-up Ukrainian attacks.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Bloch
